返回本批次

亲爱的 Heroku：呃，发生了什么事？

Dear Heroku: Uhh What's Going On?

judoscale.com 作者 crcastle 分数 54 评论 16 2026/04/07 09:42
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 亲爱的赫罗库，
  2. 作为朋友、长期市场合作伙伴、支持者和客户，我们 Judoscale 以及开发者社区的许多其他人对旧金山发生的事情感到有点困惑。除了沮丧之外，我们很难弄清楚如何看待整个“Heroku 事件”。
  3. 首先是 2 月 6 日的博客文章，突然宣布：Heroku 正在过渡到专注于稳定性、安全性、可靠性和支持的可持续工程模型……重点是保持质量和卓越运营，而不是引入新功能……
  4. 企业帐户合同将不再向新客户提供......
  5. 完全披露，这听起来很像进入“维护模式”的产品，即使没有说出具体的短语。具体来说，“可持续工程”模式到底是什么？恕我直言，这感觉就像是在公司公关会议上炮制的一句话。

Original Summary

  1. Dear Heroku,
  2. As friends, long time marketplace-partners, supporters, and customers, we here at Judoscale — along with so many others from the developer community — are a bit confused about what’s going on over there in San Francisco. Frustrations aside, we’re having a hard time figuring out what to think about this whole ‘Heroku thing’.
  3. First came the blog post on February 6th that, sort of out of nowhere, announced that: Heroku is transitioning to a sustaining engineering model focused on stability, security, reliability, and support… with an emphasis on maintaining quality and operational excellence rather than introducing new features…
  4. Enterprise Account contracts will no longer be offered to new customers…
  5. Which, full disclosure, sounds an awful lot like a product going into “Maintenance Mode”, even if that specific phrase isn’t said. Speaking of specific phrases, what exactly is a “sustaining engineering” model? Respectfully, that feels like a phrase cooked up in a corporate PR meeting.

评论（默认中文）

msteffen 2026/04/07 12:51
五块钱是这样的：

管理层：“我们将进入维护模式”

开发人员：“你的意思是我们可以做任何我们想做的事？！”

Five bucks it’s this:

Management: “we’re going into maintenance mode”

Devs: “You mean we get to work on whatever we want?!”

在 HN 中查看
msteffen 2026/04/07 13:03
下周：“我们正在调整组织规模”
Next week: “we are right-sizing the organization”
在 HN 中查看
elwray 2026/04/07 14:50
我认为Herokus和世界数字海洋不可能在云世界中生存。他们或许能够为客户创造更好的体验，但没有人可以与 AWS、GCP 和 Azure 提供的网络相媲美。低延迟总是会赢得更好的开发者体验。
I think its impossible for the Herokus and the digital oceans of the world to survive in the cloud world. They might be able to create better experience for customers but noone can match the networking that AWS, GCP and Azure can provide. Low latency will always win over better developer experience.
在 HN 中查看
arnvald 2026/04/07 16:01
Heroku 在 AWS 上运行，不是吗？他们只是把它包装起来。

我不认为他们不可能生存。 Salesforce 十多年前收购了它们，但几乎没有采取任何措施来支持 Heroku 的发展。然而它们仍然存在，人们仍然会问“是否有具有可比客户体验的新产品？”因为他们不介意多付钱

Heroku runs on AWS though, doesn’t it? They just package it.

I don’t think it’s impossible for them to survive. Salesforce bought them more than 10 years ago and did little to support growth of Heroku. And yet they’re still around and people still ask „is there something new with comparable customer experience?” because they don’t mind paying more

在 HN 中查看
interstice 2026/04/07 15:17
我从未发现云有任何东西可以击败放置良好的服务器的速度（和价格）。

不过，DO 对我来说一直有点丰富，即使是低成本的 Hetzner VPS，其核心数量也比我记得十年前以相同价格看到的要少。虽然我通常使用 Vultr 作为他们的 SYD 区域，但我可能是错的。

I've never found cloud anything to beat the speed (and price) of a well placed server.

DO has always been a bit rich for my blood though, and even a low cost hetzner VPS has less cores than I remember seeing at the same price a decade ago. I could be wrong there though I usually use Vultr for their SYD region.

在 HN 中查看
dangus 2026/04/07 13:40
博客作者不明白这一点，但它很简单：该产品只在大型企业客户的背景下才重要。

只要要求不太大，大客户仍然可以获得他们想要的东西，这就是为什么即使产品处于维护模式，你也会看到新功能。

The blog author isn’t understanding it but it’s quite simple: the product only matters in the context of large enterprise customers.

The large customers still get what they want as long as the ask isn’t too big and that’s why you see new features even though the product is in maintenance mode.

在 HN 中查看
sghiassy 2026/04/07 13:21
自从 Salesforce 收购 Heroku 以来，Heroku 一直在走下坡路。
Heroku has been going downhill ever since Salesforce bought them.
在 HN 中查看
tnolet 2026/04/07 13:46
事实上恰恰相反：收购后他们进入了鼎盛时期。可能不是由于 Salesforce，但仍然如此。
Actually the opposite: they came into their prime after the acquisition. Probably not due to Salesforce, but still.

0xc133 2026/04/07 13:34
强烈不同意。直到 2011 年，即收购后的第二年，他们甚至才发明了构建包。
Strong disagree. They didn’t even invent buildpacks until 2011, the year after the acquisition.

在 HN 中查看
nextaccountic 2026/04/07 15:17
如果他们在收购一年后仍然有动力，我认为很难说他们已经走下坡路了

也许我们可以说他们走了一段时间的上坡路？或者什么

If they still had momentum one year after acquisition, I think it's hard to say they have been going downhill

Maybe we could say they went uphill instead for a while? Or something

在 HN 中查看
9dev 2026/04/07 13:39
它只是处于昏迷状态，靠呼吸器慢慢死去。一些亲属不合理地继续向医院付费以维持患者的生命，但医生只是等到他们最终可以拔掉插头并将床位用于有实际生存机会的人。
It’s just in coma, slowly dying away on a respirator. Some relatives irrationally keep paying the hospital to keep the patient alive, but the doctors just wait until they can finally pull the plugs and use the bed for someone with actual chances of survival.
在 HN 中查看
cyberax 2026/04/07 13:43
我认为当他们推出“私人空间对等”时，下滑就开始了。它是 AWS VPC 之上的包装器，但几年前每月的费用约为 1000 美元。它还限制了更大的实例和其他重要功能。

所以很少有人用它。我想这向他们的管理层发出了关于新功能采用率的负面信号。然后一切最终都死了。

I think the downhill slide started when they introduced the "Private Space Peering". It is a wrapper on top of AWS VPC, but it was something like $1000 a month several years ago. It also was gating larger instances and other important features.

So few people used it. I guess this provided a negative signal to their management about the adoption rate of new features. And then everything eventually just died.

在 HN 中查看

Heroku 的更新

在 HN 中查看
N_Lens 2026/04/07 13:31
这是一篇多么奇怪的文章，它对 Heroku 博客文章中的语言进行了微观分析。我的意思是，如今，对于大多数公司来说，核心流失已成为常态，所以我不会对首席高管的废话抱有任何信心。
What a weird article that's microanalysing language in Heroku's blog posts. I mean times are such that pivot-churn is becoming business as usual for most outfits these days so I wouldn't put any stock on C-Suite verbiage.
在 HN 中查看
